name: National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics
theoryOfChange: NAOMCC aims to unite Muslim charitable clinics and healthcare organizations to share common interests and collaborate, providing support through fundraising navigation, education/training, advocacy, and resource pooling to improve and expand quality service delivery for underserved communities.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2023 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

amalEvaluation.charity_name: National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics

components.evidence: Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 23/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, global health cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish current reserve levels and a board-approved reserve policy (often 3-12 months for most nonprofits).
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

judge_issues.message: Religious organization (Form 990-exempt) - limited financial data available
Missing mission: Mission statement needed for donor understanding

score_details.score_summary: National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics shows moderate alignment and limited impact.

zakat.claim_evidence: The official website and descriptions of the National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics (NAOMCC) do not explicitly mention accepting zakat donations on their donation pages or in their organizational overview. While they support Muslim charitable clinics, there is no direct statement from NAOMCC itself regarding zakat acceptance. (Source: https://www.naomcc.org/Membership/)

all_citations.claim: The organization is identified as a network for uniting healthcare organizations with limited financial data available.
Financial records indicate a lack of reported revenue and expense metrics for the current period.
The organization accepts general charitable donations (sadaqah) to support its member clinic network.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a score of 39/100, reflecting a significant lack of transparent financial data and program evidence, which results in a low data confidence rating.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public disclosure regarding specific mission statements and program outcomes
Low data confidence due to missing financial metrics and independent evaluations

dimension_explanations.alignment: The alignment score of 23/50 reflects a medium level of fit; while the cause of global health is urgent, the lack of detailed organizational transparency leads to a low donor fit for those seeking high-accountability giving [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The impact score of 16/50 is considered medium-low, as the organization focuses on capacity building rather than direct service delivery, and there is currently no available data regarding cost per beneficiary or program effectiveness [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics serves as a unifying network for healthcare organizations to enhance community-based medical services.
strengths: Focuses on capacity building by uniting diverse healthcare organizations
Provides a dedicated platform for member organization support
baseline_narrative.summary: The National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics (NAOMCC) operates as a central body dedicated to uniting healthcare organizations and providing essential support for its member clinics [1]. The organization facilitates collaboration among medical providers to improve health outcomes through collective resource sharing and advocacy [3].

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization is an emerging entity (founded roughly 3 years ago) and is currently undergoing the process of establishing its reporting infrastructure.
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="15">lack of transparent financial data</cite> and the absence of a full rating from major agencies.

confidence.data_freshness: Governance data from 2025; impact data unverified
confidence.notes: Data is extremely limited due to the organization's emerging status and lack of public tax filings.

alignment.improvement: The organization could improve alignment by <cite id="20">expanding services to underserved geographies</cite> with limited nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors face challenges due to <cite id="15">low data confidence (0.05)</cite> and limited public disclosure of tax filings. However, trust is supported by <cite id="10">meeting all 20 BBB standards</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="16">publish current reserve levels</cite> and a board-approved reserve policy.

impact.explanation: The impact score of 16/50 is driven by a <cite id="7">clear theory of change</cite> but hampered by <cite id="8">missing beneficiary counts</cite> and financial health data.
impact.improvement: The organization should <cite id="17">publish beneficiary counts consistently</cite> so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Does not currently operate as a primary grantmaking entity.

rich_narrative.headline: The National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics serves as a unifying network for healthcare organizations to enhance community-based medical services.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value institutional development and want to support the infrastructure of Muslim-led healthcare clinics through sadaqah.
donor_motivations: Want to strengthen the collective voice of Muslim medical providers
Value advocacy and training over direct one-to-one aid
Interested in long-term capacity building for the healthcare sector
giving_considerations: Donations support administrative and network functions rather than direct patient bills
Financial transparency is currently limited as the organization matures
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking immediate, high-evidence humanitarian relief or those looking to fulfill zakat obligations.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects a clear strategic plan but <cite id="14">unverified outcome data</cite> and a lack of external evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: By uniting clinics and providing training, NAOMCC creates a more resilient healthcare network for underserved communities.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that capacity-building efforts actually translate into better patient outcomes at the member clinic level.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: As a young organization in a well-covered space, there is significant <cite id="21">moderate funding gap assumed</cite> to reach full operational capacity.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Focuses specifically on the <cite id="2">Muslim charitable clinic network</cite>, whereas peers often focus on direct service delivery.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focuses on clinical psychology and education
similar_organizations.name: Maristan

strengths.detail: The organization effectively <cite id="1">unites diverse healthcare organizations</cite> to enhance the collective impact of community-based medical services.
NAOMCC provides a <cite id="3">dedicated platform for member support</cite>, including critical <cite id="5">education and training</cite> programs.
The organization <cite id="10">meets all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite> for Charity Accountability, including having a <cite id="11">conflict of interest policy</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: NAOMCC's primary strength lies in its role as a force multiplier for charitable medicine. By focusing on <cite id="4">collaboration and resource pooling</cite>, it allows smaller clinics to access a wider network of expertise. This is bolstered by a <cite id="12">pass rating on governance and effectiveness</cite> from the BBB, which confirms that the organization maintains <cite id="13">audited financial statements</cite> and a structured board oversight process.
rich_narrative.summary: The National Association of Muslim Charitable Clinics (NAOMCC) serves as a <cite id="1">unifying network for healthcare organizations</cite> dedicated to enhancing community-based medical services. Operating primarily as a capacity-building entity, the organization focuses on <cite id="2">uniting diverse healthcare providers</cite> to share resources and improve health outcomes. Its core activities include providing a <cite id="3">dedicated platform for member organization support</cite>, which facilitates <cite id="4">collaboration and resource pooling</cite> among clinics that might otherwise operate in isolation. Beyond administrative support, NAOMCC engages in <cite id="5">education and training</cite> for medical staff and leads <cite id="6">advocacy efforts</cite> to represent the interests of charitable clinics at a broader level. While the organization maintains a <cite id="7">clear theory of change</cite> centered on strengthening the infrastructure of Muslim-led health initiatives, it currently operates with <cite id="8">limited public financial disclosure</cite>. As an emerging institution, its impact is measured through the <cite id="9">support of its member clinics</cite> rather than direct patient care, positioning it as a strategic intermediary in the global health space.
